academic_requirements.summary: Applicants need a bachelor's degree or equivalent foreign qualification with a minimum three-year undergraduate background and proven skills in Mathematics, especially linear algebra, differential and integral calculus, Probability and Statistics. Country-based entry-title and GPA rules also apply.
academic_requirements.prior_degree_or_diploma: Bachelor's degree or equivalent foreign qualification.
academic_requirements.subject_requirements: A minimum three-year undergraduate degree (or equivalent) with proven skills in Mathematics (linear algebra, differential and integral calculus), Probability and Statistics.
academic_requirements.gpa_or_country_requirements: Applicants must verify country-based specific entry-title and GPA requirements on the official University page linked from the application platform. No single universal GPA threshold is separately published on the reviewed programme pages.

accepted_proofs: IELTS Academic or General Training: minimum score 6.0.
Cambridge English B2 First: minimum score 173.
Trinity College London Integrated Skills in English ISE II.
Oxford Test of English: minimum score 126.
GATEHOUSE ESOL International Classic B2: GA Level 1 Certificate.
Pearson Test of English General: PTE General 3.
Pearson English International Certificate: Level 3 Upper Intermediate.
Pearson Test of English Academic: minimum score 65.
exemption_rules: Applicants are exempt if their previous education leading to the entry qualification was completed entirely in English.
Applicants are exempt if they are native English speakers from one of the countries listed by the University of Padua.
The IMAT exemption mentioned by the University applies only to Medicine and Surgery, not to this programme.

application_deadline_eu.notes: Call Three is only for EU students and non-EU students residing in Italy.

application_deadline_non_eu.call_three: Not applicable for non-EU applicants residing outside Italy.
application_deadline_non_eu.notes: The third call is restricted to EU students and non-EU students residing in Italy.

required_documents.notes: Upload a colour scanned PDF. If needed, provide an English or Italian translation. No legalised documents are required at application stage.

required_documents.document_name: Academic qualification or provisional enrolment certificate
required_documents.notes: If the final qualification has not yet been awarded, an enrolment or provisional certificate is accepted.
EU citizens and non-EU citizens residing in Italy may use an ID card; other applicants normally provide a passport.
Required only for non-EU applicants residing in Italy.

required_documents.document_name: English language certificate or other accepted proof
required_documents.notes: Required unless the applicant qualifies for an official exemption.
The University asks applicants to check the entry requirements of the chosen programme for any additional required document.

required_documents.document_name: Bachelor's diploma translated into Italian or English and legalised
required_documents.notes: Required after admission to complete enrolment; a CIMEA Statement of Verification can be used as an alternative in the cases stated by the University.

required_documents.document_name: Transcript of Records translated into Italian or English and legalised
required_documents.notes: The transcript must be issued by the competent academic authority and show passed exams, grades, and programme description.

required_documents.document_name: Universitaly pre-enrolment and study visa D - enrolment
required_documents.notes: Non-EU citizens residing outside Italy must pre-enrol through Universitaly no later than 15 July and obtain a study visa.

required_documents.document_name: Residence permit or residence-permit application receipt
required_documents.notes: Required for non-EU applicants who are in Italy at enrolment stage.

entry_exam_or_test.test_name: No standard written entry test published
entry_exam_or_test.scoring_or_threshold: The official pages reviewed describe admission through verification of entry requirements and application review, but do not publish a separate written test or interview for this programme.

source_quotes.quote: A minimum three-year undergraduate degree (or equivalent) with proven skills in Mathematics (linear algebra, differential and integral calculus), Probability and Statistics
31st August for open access degree programmes.
Call Three: 2 June 2026 - 2 August 2026 (only for EU students and non-EU students residing in Italy)
